name: Armstrong Number
description: Given a positive integer n, check whether it is an Armstrong number. An Armstrong number (narcissistic number) of d digits is a number where the sum of each digit raised to the power d equals the number itself. For example, 153 = 1^3 + 5^3 + 3^3.

name: Check Palindrome Number
description: Given an integer x, return true if x is a palindrome, and false otherwise. An integer is a palindrome when it reads the same forward and backward. Negative numbers are not palindromes. Solve it without converting the integer to a string.

name: Count Primes
description: Given an integer n, return the number of prime numbers that are strictly less than n. Use the Sieve of Eratosthenes for O(n log log n) solution.

name: LCM and GCD
description: Given two positive integers a and b, compute both their Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) and Least Common Multiple (LCM). Use the Euclidean algorithm for GCD: gcd(a, b) = gcd(b, a % b). LCM can be derived as: lcm(a, b) = (a * b) / gcd(a, b).
